avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Localised areas of windslab will continue to gradually develop through the day. Areas most affected will be steep wind sheltered areas such as gullies and coire rims with a South-West through West to North aspect above 1000 metres. Fragile cornices will develop on these aspects. Elsewhere the existing snowpack will be well bonded and stable.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Showers starting just before dawn will fall as snow above around 600 metres and continue through the day. Gale force East-South-Easterly winds continuing becoming mainly Easterly as the day progresses. The freezing level will fall to around 600 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Showers in the morning fell as snow above around 950 metres followed by a drier afternoon. Gale force South-Easterly winds across the summits. The freezing level lowered gradually through the day to around 900 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack remains well bonded and stable. Localised areas of fresh unstable windslab are developing on some steep windsheltered areas on West to North aspects above 1100 metres. Gullies and coire rims are most affected. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Unstable windslab will develop as the day progresses on West to North aspects above 900 metres. North-East aspects will be subject to cross loading. Coire headwalls, crag aprons and steep wind sheltered locations will be most affected. At lower elevations steep banks and burn lines may be subject to isolated accumulations. Elsewhere above 1000 metres weaknesses remain present beneath a widespread friable crust. Fragile cornices will continue to develop. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Precipitation is expected throughout the period, this falling initially as snow above 700 metres, later above 400 metres. Winds will be from the East-South-East and very strong to gale force. The freezing level will gradually fall from 800 metres to 600 metres by the end of the period.
Observed Weather Influences: Persistent snow showers above 1000 metres. Winds were South-easterly, very strong to gale force during the day. The freezing level was around 900 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh unstable windslab was present on West through North-West to North-East aspects above 1000 metres, particularly around coire rims and in gully tops. Avalanche and cornice debris was observed below these aspects. Elsewhere, the snowpack has a widely developed friable crust above 1000 metres, with weaknesses observed below. Large fragile cornices remain around coire rims of the same aspects as above. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Older snow will become firm and icy with new windslab developing in wind sheltered locations mainly on West to North aspects above 900 metres. Isolated unstable deposits will also be found on South and South-West aspects with lee locations at lower elevations also affected. Steep sheltered terrain such as corrie rims, gully tops, scarp slopes, convexities and burn lines will be most affected. Any newly formed cornices will be fragile.  The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The freezing level will drop to around 600 metres by the end of the day. Snowfall is expected throughout, heaviest showers overnight. Winds will be gale force, mainly from the East.
Observed Weather Influences: The freezing level was around 900 metres overnight,  lowering slightly for most of the day. Flurries of light snow and sleet were noted above 800 metres. Winds were very strong, mainly from the East-South-East.
ObservedSnowStability: The majority of the snowpack continues to consolidate.  Instabilities persist within deeper deposits mainly found on South-West through West to North-East aspects above 1000 metres. At lower elevations snow continues to thaw with snow loss noted. Cornice stability remains suspect.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Fresh deposits of unstable windslab will progressively build on South-West through West to North aspects above 900 metres. Wind-sheltered terrain such as coire rims and gully tops will be most affected. Cornices will build above the same aspects. Outwith these areas, stability will be good. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The 900 metre freezing level will drop steadily to reach 600 metres by the end of the forecasting period. Precipitation expected throughout the forecasting period, initially falling as snow above 750, later above 500 metres. Very strong to gale force winds will be East-South-Easterly overnight, backing East from dawn.
Observed Weather Influences: Light snowfall near the summits, rain at lower elevations. The freezing level was initially just below the summits, later dropping to around 900 metres. Strong to very strong East-South-Easterly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is mostly moist but generally stable throughout. Only limited drifting of new snow evident above 950 metres. Existing cornices are weak. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Areas of fresh windslab will build on South-West to North-West aspects above 700 meters in the East of the region. These deposits are expected to be localised and easily avoidable. Areas affected will be gully tops, corrie rims and the leeward side of ridges. Mountains in the West of the region will be unaffected and the snowpack here will remain well bonded and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force Easterly winds. Light snow showers expected in the East of the region, predominantly dry across the coastal ranges. The freezing level will lower to around 600 meters during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Strong Easterly winds. Light showers across the East of the region, predominantly dry in the West. The freezing level was on or just above summit level.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable throughout. Only patchy cover now remains, mainly above 700 meters increasing in extent with height. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will begin to refreeze at all elevations with a thin breakable crust forming. Localised deposits of unstable windslab will form in steep wind sheltered locations on mainly Westerly aspects above 850 metres. These localised accumulations are expected to be shallow, mainly around gully tops, coire rims and ridge flanks. Small cornices may begin to form on these aspects also. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The cold Easterly airflow continues to bring cloudy and damp conditions. Light rain from the early hours and through the day will fall as snow above around 500 metres. Easterly winds will be fresh to strong throughout the period.
Observed Weather Influences: Damp and cloudy with outbreaks of rain and snow above around 950 metres. Feeling cold with moderate to fresh East-South-East winds.
ObservedSnowStability: At all elevations the existing snowpack is soft and moist with generally good stability. A very thin breakable crust has formed above around 1000 metres with pockets of reasonably bonded soft snow and windslab forming on mainly Westerly aspects, though amounts are shallow and not significant. At lower elevations the snowpack is thawing and diminishing. The avalanche hazard is Low.
